it only sets up contract instances.\n- The actual security risk depends on how this factory is used; however, as standalone code, it is safe.\n\n**Potential vulnerabilities or assumptions:**\n\n- No assumptions about vulnerabilities are made; the code is standard.\n- No network calls or external data handling beyond the contract connection are present.\n\n**Consistency of scores:**\n\n- The scores are consistent with the code's nature and the reports' reasoning.\n\n---\n\n### Final assessment:\n\n**Malware score:** 0 — The code is a benign, autogenerated factory with no malicious intent.\n\n**Obfuscated score:** 0 — The code is straightforward, with no obfuscation.\n\n**Risk score:** 0 — As a utility class, it does not pose security risks; actual risks depend on usage context.\n\n**Additional notes:**\n\n- Given the reports and the code, I agree with the assigned scores.\n- The code's purpose is limited to contract interfacing; it does not perform any sensitive operations itself.\n\n**Summary:**\n\nThis supply chain report accurately characterizes the code as safe, standard, and free of malware or obfuscation.
